description_long: This object is a vintage-style television with a boxy, rectangular wooden exterior and a prominent, convex glass screen on the front. The front face features a large monochrome display with the word 'Sketchfab' printed across it, surrounded by a thick, dark bezel. To the right of the screen, there are several circular dials and colored buttons, likely for channel and volume control, as well as power and input selection. The sides and back of the television are finished in a woodgrain texture, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The back is solid and unadorned, with no visible vents or ports. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century analog televisions commonly found in urban homes, garages, or curbside for disposal.
description: A retro, box-shaped television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and side control dials and buttons. The front features a large monochrome display and classic analog controls.
description_view_0: The left side of the television shows the woodgrain texture and the boxy, angular shape, with no visible controls or screen.
description_view_1: The front view displays the convex glass screen with 'Sketchfab' text, thick bezel, and a vertical row of dials and colored buttons on the right.
description_view_2: The right side mirrors the left, showing the woodgrain finish and the angular, protruding back, with no controls or screen visible.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a solid, flat woodgrain panel with no visible ports, vents, or controls.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, black, matte-finished bezel with a rectangular shape and no visible branding
attribute_control_button_layout: vertical cluster of dials and buttons on the right front panel
